headline: #Optimization modes
summary: Select Auto, then choose how the router optimizes:

headline: #Admin controls
summary: Admins can enable the router per team or group, restrict which optimization modes members can use, set the default mode, and allow or block underlying models.

headline: Improvements↓↑
summary: Jul 17, 2026·Changelog

headline: #Interaction improvements
summary: Cursor now responds with a plan before it begins, so you can jump in and redirect early. As it works, it updates its status so you can follow each step.

headline: #Multi-repo environment support
summary: From Slack, Cursor can now start in a named multi-repo environment instead of a single default repository. If your frontend, backend, and shared code live in separate repos, Cursor reads your request and targets the environment that gives it access to all of them.

headline: #Cross-channel workflows
summary: Cursor can now read from and send messages to other Slack channels and threads. During a task, it can pull context from elsewhere in the workspace and post updates back in the original thread or the relevant channel.

headline: #Side chats
summary: Open a side chat to ask questions, explore ideas, and investigate tangents without interrupting your main agent conversation. Use/side,/btw, or the plus button at the top of the chat panel to create a new side chat that has context from the main chat.

headline: #Conversation search
summary: Find past agent chats faster with search results that go beyond names and PR numbers. In theAgents Window, you can search agent transcripts from the command palette (Cmd+K). Cursor builds a local search index that scales search to thousands of conversations with snappy performance.

headline: #Redesigned project and repo pickers
summary: We've simplified the project and repo pickers and made them more powerful. You can now stay in the picker for workflows that used to send you elsewhere. For example, you can create a project and connect GitHub, GitLab, or Azure DevOps without leaving the picker.

headline: #Team MCPs in team marketplaces
summary: Admins can now configure Team MCP servers once and distribute them across cloud agents, the agents window, IDE, and CLI.

headline: #Marketplace access by organization group
summary: Team marketplaces now supportorganization groups, in addition to team-level SCIM directory groups.

headline: #Cloud agents on mobile
summary: Open the Cursor mobile app, choose a repo, and launch an agent the same way you would on the desktop app. Pick any frontier model, describe ideas out loud with voice input, and use slash commands to guide Cursor in the right direction.

headline: #Remote Control
summary: UseRemote Controlto take an agent you're running on your computer and keep directing it from your phone.

headline: #Live Activities and push notifications
summary: Track the status of your agents with Live Activities on your lock screen. Get push notifications when an agent finishes, needs input, or is ready for review.

headline: #Marketplace leaderboard
summary: Cursor now shows you a leaderboard of the most popular plugins, skills, and MCPs across your team.

headline: #Plugin canvases
summary: Plugins now support prebuilt canvases: shared setup templates your team can open and reuse.

headline: #New Team Marketplaces
summary: Team marketplacesnow support imports of plugin repositories from GitLab, BitBucket, or Azure DevOps so you easily add plugins and distribute them to your team.

headline: Documentation Index
summary: Fetch the complete documentation index at: /docs/llms.txt
